title: Moist Molasses Canadian Salmon Skewers
description: A citrusy and flavorful Canadian dish featuring molasses and salmon, perfect for a date night, this recipe is also Buddhist-temple-friendly as it does not include any animal by-products or alcohol, and is made with brine-wet salmon for a moist texture.
instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high heat, about 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together molasses, orange juice, olive oil, lemon zest, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the salmon pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the molasses mixture.
Thread the salmon pieces onto the bamboo skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Place the skewers on the grill and cook for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until the salmon is cooked through.
Remove the skewers from the grill and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Let the skewers rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the juices to redistribute.
